matches.moment: Haaland draws a foul from Diomandé inside the area — Norway convert from the spot and Ivory Coast's compact defensive shape opens as they chase the game

Lindelöf, covering at CB for injured Hien, fails to cut Mbappé's lateral run at the edge of the box in the 22nd minute — Mbappé cuts inside and finishes low to the near post; Sweden's shape collapses immediately

Ecuador goalkeeper Galíndez hesitates on a crossed corner in the 31st minute under the deafening Azteca crowd roar — the ball drops to Giménez unmarked at the six-yard box; communication between Galíndez and Pacho breaks down in the noise

verdict: June 30 is the day the Round of 32 begins to separate tournament contenders from hopeful survivors. Norway at full strength — Haaland, Ødegaard, Nusa all returning from rest — is the single clearest upgrade in the day's slate: their xG/shot of 0.18 (highest in the tournament) paired with Haaland's 4 goals from 2 full-strength appearances marks them as genuine dark-horse semi-finalists, and Ivory Coast's defense, which yielded 1.83 xG to Germany, has not been tested by a forward of this caliber. France's destruction of Sweden will be methodical: their 1.77 xG/game average against competitive opponents is solid, and Sweden's Hien-depleted defense (Lindelöf covering at CB for the first time at this tournament) collapses the margin the Swedes need — Gyökeres and Isak will threaten once, as they always do, but 3-1 France is consistent with xG reality. The most analytically interesting match is at the Azteca: Mexico's 1.23 xG/game (lowest of today's predicted winners) against Ecuador's 0.70 adjusted xG, with the +0.35 Azteca crowd adjustment and Ecuador's conversion failure (0 goals in 2 of 3 WC games) supporting the 2-0. Ecuador's defensive spine of Pacho-Hincapié-Caicedo is the best defensive unit on the field today — the margins are thinner than the scorelines suggest. — The Analyst.

players.name: Erling Haaland
players.stat: 4 WC goals in 2 full-strength games; xG/shot 0.18 (highest in tournament)
players.watch: First 30 minutes — if he gets in behind Diomandé, the game is settled early

players.name: Nicolas Pépe
players.stat: 2 WC goals — tournament's leading Ivory Coast scorer
players.watch: Any Norway lead forces Ivory Coast open on the counter, which is Pépe's optimal environment

players.name: Kylian Mbappé
players.stat: 4 WC goals, 6 goal involvements across 3 games
players.watch: Diagonal runs across Lindelöf (now at CB) in the first 20 minutes; early goal ends the match as a contest

players.name: Viktor Gyökeres
players.stat: 1 WC goal (vs Tunisia); Arsenal's leading scorer in 2025/26 (fallback)
players.watch: Second-ball situations in the French box in the 70-90 minute window

players.name: Moisés Caicedo
players.stat: Key midfield anchor; no injury concerns; screens for Pacho-Hincapié CB pair
players.watch: Late-game discipline — a booking when chasing a deficit could suspend him for the R16
